text.title: Solo Go No Go Criteria Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Solo Go No Go Criteria. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Gate uses ONLY 5 questions: build quality, scope vs promise, comms ready, rollback tested, metric instrumented.
rule.rationale: Gate uses ONLY 5 questions: build quality, scope vs promise, comms ready, rollback tested, metric instrumented.

rule.statement: Owner signs the go / hold / no-op record; unsigned = no ship.
rule.rationale: Owner signs the go / hold / no-op record; unsigned = no ship.

rule.statement: No 'go IF we add X tomorrow' — gate is binary at ship moment.
rule.rationale: No 'go IF we add X tomorrow' — gate is binary at ship moment.

rule.statement: Rollback runbook tested in staging within 7 days of ship.
rule.rationale: Rollback runbook tested in staging within 7 days of ship.

rule.statement: Success metric + threshold is named before ship.
rule.rationale: Success metric + threshold is named before ship.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-solo-go-no-go-criteria.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
